stat_block.text: The weapon wielded in battle by Szuriel, Rider of War, is a perpetually blood-covered blade that has taken more lives than even the gods can count. *Lamentation of the Faithless* is a *+4 major striking speed unholy greatsword* with a blade of unyielding jet-black steel that absorbs all incident light, counteracting all light effects in a 15-foot radius with a +37 modifier as long as the weapon is unsheathed. Any creature within range attempting to use a spell or ability with the light trait must first succeed at a DC 15 flat check, or the attempt fails. Whenever the blade strikes and deals damage to a living creature, its wielder regains 1d12 Hit Points.

traits.name: Unique
traits.text: A rules element with this trait is one-of-a-kind. The DC of Recall Knowledge checks related to creatures with this trait is increased by 10.

traits.name: Artifact
traits.text: Items with this trait are artifacts. These magic items can't be crafted by normal means, and they can't be damaged by normal means. Artifacts are always rare or unique.

traits.name: Divine
traits.text:
This magic comes from the divine tradition, drawing power from deities or similar sources. Anything with this trait is magical.  
  
A creature with this trait is primarily constituted of or has a strong connection to divine magic.

traits.name: Magical
traits.text: Something with the magical trait is imbued with magical energies not tied to a specific tradition of magic. Some items or effects are closely tied to a particular tradition of magic. In these cases, the item has the arcane, divine, occult, or primal trait instead of the magical trait. Any of these traits indicate that the item is magical.

traits.name: Unholy
traits.text: Effects with the unholy trait are tied to powerful magical forces of cruelty and sin. They often have stronger effects on holy creatures. Creatures with this trait are strongly devoted to unholy causes, and often have weakness to holy. If a creature with weakness to unholy uses an unholy item or effect, it takes damage from its weakness.

abilities.name: Activate
abilities.effect: You raise  *Lamentation of the Faithless* high above the battlefield, presenting its terrible glory for all to witness. All creatures within 60 feet that are able to see the sword must attempt a DC 50 Will save. Creatures with the unholy trait are immune to this effect, but creatures with the holy trait take a –2 status penalty on this save.
abilities.success: The creature is stricken by crushing despair. It becomes slowed 1 as it sobs uncontrollably, and it can’t use reactions while it’s slowed.
abilities.failure: As success, but the creature is also blinded for 1 round.
abilities.critical_failure: As failure, but the duration of the blinded condition is 1 minute.
abilities.destruction: *Lamentation of the Faithless* can only be destroyed if it’s driven into the ground within the walls of the secluded garden at the summit of Heaven’s mountain by a risen, repentant fiend , whereupon it shatters and crumbles to dust.

traits.name: Manipulate
traits.text: You must physically manipulate an item or make gestures to use an action with this trait. Creatures without a suitable appendage can’t perform actions with this trait. Manipulate actions often trigger reactions.

traits.name: Unholy
traits.text: Effects with the unholy trait are tied to powerful magical forces of cruelty and sin. They often have stronger effects on holy creatures. Creatures with this trait are strongly devoted to unholy causes, and often have weakness to holy. If a creature with weakness to unholy uses an unholy item or effect, it takes damage from its weakness.

traits.name: Visual
traits.text: A visual effect can affect only creatures that can see it. This applies only to visible parts of the effect, as determined by the GM.
